name: hibiki
description: 響 — resonance born from nothing. From a seed, cast resonant objects (bell, singing bowl, glass, wood, stone, gong): the material sets the inharmonicity and decay of the overtones, the size sets the pitch. Struck, each rings with a living timbre — no samples, pure modal synthesis (a sum of decaying sinusoids). Pitches quantise to a pentatonic scale, so any objects sounding together stay consonant. Zero dependencies, offline, deterministic.
